mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in the effective federal funds rate over the prior 3 months raises the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically compressing valuation multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 1.15% in Oct-2017 amid ongoing Fed hiking cycle
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS 3-month change and forward 20d SPX return stays below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window, or positive-return share in rising-rate periods exceeds unconditional base rate of 65.5%.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a rolling 3-month window, it signals an inflection toward labor-market deterioration that historically precedes broader earnings downgrades, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.1% Oct-2017, near cycle lows
failure_condition: Following UNRATE upticks meeting the trigger, 60-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or effect is not statistically distinct from unconditional mean at p>0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) reflects broad-based consumer and residential investment strength, jointly signaling robust aggregate demand that supports risk appetite and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next month, an effect stronger than either series alone.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3m annualized growth > 3% AND HOUST 3m change > 0
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS vintage 486553M Oct-2017 rising, HOUST 1127K Sep-2017
failure_condition: Joint-trigger sample shows Rank IC < 0.03 or fails to outperform single-series RSAFS-only signal by more than noise over a 3-year rolling walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A positive month-over-month change in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects expanding manufacturing capacity utilization, a leading indicator of near-term corporate revenue growth that produces positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX return correlation with INDPRO monthly change remains below 0.02 Rank IC across a 2-year rolling walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions when CPI inflation is accelerating and the Fed is simultaneously raising rates, the joint tightening signal (rising CPIAUCSL plus rising FEDFUNDS) indicates policy is turning restrictive faster than growth can absorb, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter that are stronger than either variable predicts alone.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL 246.639 Oct-2017 vintage, FEDFUNDS 1.15% amid hiking path
failure_condition: Joint-trigger periods show 60-day forward returns not significantly different from unconditional distribution (p>0.10), or positive-return share exceeds 60%.

mechanism_narrative: A deceleration in nonfarm payroll (PAYEMS) month-over-month growth below its trailing 12-month average signals weakening labor demand ahead of consensus expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month as markets reprice growth expectations.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS MoM change falls below trailing 12-month average by > 20%
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS vintage 147010K Oct-2017 with revision risk noted as high
failure_condition: Triggered deceleration episodes produce positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of cases, or effect size is not statistically distinguishable from base rate at p>0.10.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening cycle, a sustained decline in housing starts (HOUST) reflects rate-sensitive demand contraction that historically foreshadows broader economic slowdown and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter, an effect absent in easing or neutral regimes.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3m decline > 5% while FEDFUNDS is rising
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST vintage 1127K Sep-2017 amid gradual Fed hikes
failure_condition: During FEDFUNDS-rising periods, HOUST declines fail to precede negative 60-day SPX returns in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in industrial production (INDPRO) and nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates synchronized supply-side and labor-market expansion, a combined signal of broadening economic momentum that produces positive S&P 500 returns over the following week beyond what either series predicts individually.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO 104.6367 and PAYEMS 147010K both rising as of Oct-2017 vintage
failure_condition: Joint-trigger sample fails to outperform INDPRO-only signal in walk-forward Rank IC by a margin greater than noise over 2-year rolling windows.
